product.name: ArWoWear T21 Flame Pro
product.categorySourceGroup: Flame Retardant, Vat Dyed, Workwear Fabrics Resistant up to 100 Wash
product.titleDescriptor: 80% Cotton 20% Polyester, Flame Retardant Fabric , 330 gr/m'2
product.specLine: 330 g/m², 80% Cotton 20% Polyester, VAT Dyed, Flame Retardant (100 Wash Resistant), 3/1S Twill Fabric
descriptions: ArWoWear T21 Flame Pro is heavy weight 330 g/m², 80% cotton 20% polyester, vat dyed, flame retardant workwear fabric.
It has flame retardant function with EN ISO 11612 and EN ISO 11611 certification.
It is durable up to 100 washing.
80% Cotton 20% Polyester, Flame Retardant Fabric , 330 gr/m'2
330 g/m², 80% Cotton 20% Polyester, VAT Dyed, Flame Retardant (100 Wash Resistant), 3/1S Twill Fabric
technicalParameters: Tensile Strength | Warp>1200N; Weft>1000N | EN ISO 13934-1
Tear Strength | Warp>25N; Weft>20N | EN ISO 13937-2
Dimensional Change | -3% < Length < +3% ; -3% < Width < +3% | EN ISO 5077
Dry Cleaning Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 D01
Hot Press Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 X11
Perspiration Light Fast. | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 B07
Azo Test | No Azo Colorants. | EN ISO 14362-1
Flame and Heat Protection | Yes | EN ISO 11612

acceptedMatches.rationale: Product explicitly states welding protection certified to EN ISO 11611 and lists molten-metal splash resistance, directly matching metalworking/welding protective fabric use.

possibleButNotAccepted.reason: Explicit arc-flash protection rating (ATPV, arc class) under EN 61482 directly supports electrical/high-voltage protective use associated with the energy sector.
Fabric is flame retardant and heat-protective per EN ISO 11612, but no explicit firefighter/turnout-gear use is stated.
No explicit military, police or uniformed-service use mentioned despite durable FR workwear construction.

primary.rationale: The fabric is explicitly certified to EN ISO 11611 for welding protection and lists molten-metal splash resistance, indicating a direct, stated intended use in metalworking/welding protective clothing.

secondary.rationale: The fabric also carries explicit arc-flash protection ratings (ATPV, arc class) under EN 61482, which directly supports electrical/high-voltage protective use independent of the welding application.

alternativesConsidered.reasonNotSelected: Certified to EN ISO 11612 for flame/heat protection, but no explicit statement of firefighter or turnout-gear use; this is a generic FR workwear standard, not sufficient alone per rule 9.
While the fabric is general FR workwear, the explicit welding and arc-flash certifications point to a more specific named sector, making welding the more direct primary application.
No explicit military, police or uniformed-service use is stated in the product data.

While the fabric is general FR workwear, the explicit welding and arc-flash certifications point to a more specific named sector, making welding the more direct primary application.
No explicit military, police or uniformed-service use is stated in the product data.
